mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear periods with a t-stat > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real sales of new vehicles and rising federal funds rates indicate demand destruction in rate-sensitive consumer sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction that forces monetary authorities to halt tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain negative during industrial production declines, or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive GDP revisions, or rolling IC < 0.01 over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to improve classification accuracy over a naive baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic resilience, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals collapsing consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are positive in >55% of triggered periods, or the signal loses predictive power when mortgage rates fall below 4%.
